---
title: "Markdown Cheat Sheet"
format: html
---

Every Markdown feature you're likely to need on GitHub, with the raw
syntax on the left and how it actually renders on the right.

---

## Headings

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3
#### Heading 4
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3
#### Heading 4
:::
:::

---

## Emphasis

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
**bold text**
*italic text*
***bold and italic***
~~strikethrough~~
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

**bold text**

*italic text*

***bold and italic***

~~strikethrough~~
:::
:::

---

## Blockquotes

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
> This is a quote.
>
> It can span multiple lines.
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

> This is a quote.
>
> It can span multiple lines.
:::
:::

---

## Unordered lists

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
- First item
- Second item
- Third item
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

- First item
- Second item
- Third item
:::
:::

---

## Ordered lists

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step
:::
:::

---

## Nested lists

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
- Module 1
  - Concepts
  - GitHub basics
- Module 2
  - Local Git
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

- Module 1
  - Concepts
  - GitHub basics
- Module 2
  - Local Git
:::
:::

---

## Task lists (GitHub-flavored)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
- [x] Create GitHub account
- [x] Write README
- [ ] Open a Pull Request
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

- [x] Create GitHub account
- [x] Write README
- [ ] Open a Pull Request
:::
:::

---

## Links

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
[link text](https://github.com)

[link with title](https://github.com "GitHub")

<https://github.com>
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

[link text](https://github.com)

[link with title](https://github.com "GitHub")

<https://github.com>
:::
:::

---

## Reference-style links

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
Check out [GitHub][gh] for more.

[gh]: https://github.com
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

Check out [GitHub][gh] for more.

[gh]: https://github.com
:::
:::

---

## Images

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
![Git logo](../assets/images/head-pointer.png)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

![Git logo](../assets/images/head-pointer.png){width="60%"}
:::
:::

---

## Inline code

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
Run `git status` to check your repository.
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

Run `git status` to check your repository.
:::
:::

---

## Fenced code blocks

::::: {.columns}
:::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**

`````markdown
```python
def hello():
    print("hello")
```
`````
::::
:::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

```python
def hello():
    print("hello")
```
::::
:::::

---

## Tables

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
| Module | Topic       |
|--------|-------------|
| 1      | Concepts    |
| 2      | Local Git   |
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

| Module | Topic       |
|--------|-------------|
| 1      | Concepts    |
| 2      | Local Git   |
:::
:::

---

## Table alignment

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
| Left | Center | Right |
|:-----|:------:|------:|
| a    | b      | c     |
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

| Left | Center | Right |
|:-----|:------:|------:|
| a    | b      | c     |
:::
:::

---

## Horizontal rule

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
Above the line

---

Below the line
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

Above the line

---

Below the line
:::
:::

---

## Line breaks

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
First line.\
Second line, no blank line between.
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

First line.\
Second line, no blank line between.
:::
:::

---

## Footnotes (GitHub-flavored)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
Git was created in 2005[^1].

[^1]: By Linus Torvalds.
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered**

Git was created in 2005[^1].

[^1]: By Linus Torvalds.
:::
:::

---

## Emoji shortcodes (GitHub-flavored)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Raw**
```markdown
Nice work! :tada: :rocket:
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendered** (as GitHub displays it)

Nice work! 🎉 🚀
:::
:::

---

## Mentions and issue references (GitHub only)

These only become clickable links when viewed **on GitHub.com** — they
render as plain text everywhere else (including this page).

```markdown
@dktanwar please review

See issue #12 for details
```

::: {.callout-note}
On GitHub, `@username` notifies that person, and `#12` automatically links
to issue or PR number 12 in the same repository.
:::
